name: The Trustee for Life Eternal Trust Australia
alignment_notes: Net assets: $6,621,070; Investment income: $1,369
how_purposes_pursued: Life Eternal Trust Australia promotes health and personal wellbeing to Australian population through practice of Sahaja Yoga Meditation via regular classes and workshops held in every state around Australia. The classes and workshops are conducted and provided free of cost on a weekly basis where participants learn how to overcome the stresses of modern life and clear the attention of any problems. This in turn has benefits on many levels personally, and in turn contributes to the overall wellbeing of the wider community. We also provide ongoing programs for youth and children interested in meditation. Our members also attend a number of local community events such as fairs, festivals etc. where we offer the public an opportunity to experience meditation and a chance to learn about it and become proficient in the technique involved. Proponents using the techniques report they find it much easier to relax and their general enjoyment of life dramatically improves. Sahaja Yoga Meditation has been recognised for its many benefits by medical professionals, and respected universities have confirmed the benefits of the meditation technique published clinical trials. Further information is available at www.freemeditation.com.au

name: Foundation Of Joana De Cusa Incorporated
alignment_notes: Net assets: $101,106
how_purposes_pursued: Our charity has been providing assistance to Brazilian nationals and other portuguese speaking individuals specially who have migrated to Australia. Our efforts in offering emotional support, clothing, and basic necessities like toiletries and kitchen items can make a significant difference in helping these individuals settle into their new lives and communities. Supporting immigrants in their transition to a new country can be a crucial service, as it can be a challenging and sometimes overwhelming experience for them. By offering these essential items and emotional support, we aim to contribute to their wellbeing and help them integrate into Australian society more smoothly. We expand our charity's efforts to serve a broader range of individuals in the community, reflecting a strong commitment to inclusivity and social support. By creating a safe space for everyone, including young mothers, survivors of domestic violence, and anyone in need, we aim to address a wide range of important social issues. Our charity not only provides essential support services but also engages in community activities like group studies of the gospel. These types of activities can have a positive impact on individuals and families, both spiritually and socially. We believe in the benefits of offering group studies in multiple languages and for different age groups. We take a holistic approach to community support, addressing not only material needs but also spiritual and social wellbeing. This can contribute significantly to the overall health and resilience of the community we serve.

name: The Shepherd and Mother of Light
also_known_as: THE SHEPHERD & MOTHER OF LIGHT TRUST
all_directors: Mervat Fanous, Ramy Mansour, Rimon Ghaly
alignment_notes: Grants outside AU: $860,000; Net assets: $15,075; Investment income: $1,431
intl_details: SML was established by a group of Coptic servants in 1994. Its services are exclusively dedicated to those families that live in extreme poverty on an income of less than 10 cents per day. SML s goal is to lift the families from living below the poverty line to a humane level of existence where they can have their basic needs met.
how_purposes_pursued: Our Mission  To lift the families from living below the poverty line to a humane level of existence where they can have their basic needs met. SML Main programs 1 Social Services Program  Our Lord Jesus Christ who says  For I was hungry and you gave Me food; I was thirsty and you gave Me drink; I was a stranger and you took Me in; I was naked and you clothed Me; I was sick and you visited Me; I was in prison and you came to Me. Assuredly, I say to you, inasmuch as you did it to one of the least of these My brethren, you did it to Me.  Matthew 25 In our routine monthly visits, SML servants check the needs of the families they visit, in addition to seasonal campaigns to provide clothes and food in Christmas and Easter. We just want to put joyful smiles on the faces of these needy kids and their families.  2 Housing Program  A fruit of love that gives special care to help families live in healthy and secured homes with basic needs like clean running tap water, electricity, bathrooms, installation of roofs, windows and doors. Some houses are rebuilt entirely, others are renovated according to the decision of a committee of professional engineers.  3 Economic Development Program  One that is designed to empower the beneficiaries and transform them from a dependent status to an independent one. Ensuring they become productive members in their communities either to get them jobs through employment offices, or to give them interest free loans to start Income Generating Projects.  4 Education Program A fruit of love that aims to provide a chance in education for a better future through schooling and university. * Preschool and Kindergarten classes to provide better care and basic education for a kick start into primary school.  * Literacy and Adult Education Centers   They learn how to read and write, gain awareness of healthy habits and hygiene, recognize their rights and responsibilities as citizens, and develop skills to improve their economic status.  * Tutoring classes to encourage our children to excel in education. We want them to stand out.  * University Student Sponsorship for excelling students offering a variety of degrees at top universities, and following through until their graduation.    5 Special Needs Program  A fruit of love towards the disabled sons and daughters of Jesus to help them attain their rights in the community and work to enhance their selfesteem and selfactualization through: * Education Centers  * Vocational Training and workshops     6 Spiritual Program  A fruit of love that aims to strengthen the spiritual life of the beneficiaries through  * Coordination with the local church  * Strong Christian family programs  * King s daughter program  * Conduction of spiritual days and retreats 7 Health Program A fruit of love Ensuring appropriate medical care for the sick by connecting them with specialists for examination and treatment ranging from medications to surgeries.
